postText: A new data center proposed in Indianapolis would include 56 backup diesel generators and be located directly across the street from an elementary school 😱 https://t.co/5RJOhIf0SR
keyFindings: Each data center building requires approximately 56 generators for emergency power, highlighting the high infrastructure demand for data center reliability.
Backup systems are designed with fuel storage for a 48-hour runtime at 100% load to ensure operational continuity during extended outages.
Noise and community impact are managed by placing generators in insulated enclosures and restricting testing to between 7:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. on weekdays.
The project commits to environmental standards through a Spill Prevention, Control and Countermeasure (SPCC) plan and adherence to IDEM air permit limits.

postText: A new data center proposed in Indianapolis would include 56 backup diesel generators and be located directly across the street from an elementary school 😱 https://t.co/5RJOhIf0SR
keyFindings: The data center campus consists of three buildings with a total combined floor area of approximately 417,197 square feet.
Building 3 is the largest structure on site, totaling 194,708 square feet over two floors with a building height of 56 feet.
The site design integrates significant energy infrastructure, including an existing substation easement to support high-density power requirements.
Proposed infrastructure includes equipment yards and a proposed 8-foot tall soil berm, likely intended for noise mitigation of cooling and power systems.
The development requires a total of 251 parking stalls, with Building 2 and Building 3 requesting a 50% variance for parking requirements.

postText: A new data center proposed in Indianapolis would include 56 backup diesel generators and be located directly across the street from an elementary school 😱 https://t.co/5RJOhIf0SR
keyFindings: A linear distance of 675.15 feet is measured from a point near Irvington Community Elementary School to a target location marked by a red pin.
The measurement path crosses the Pennsy Trail, which may serve as a corridor for utility easements or public access.
The proximity of the target location to an educational facility (approximately 675 feet) is a critical constraint for energy infrastructure siting or environmental assessments.
The site is located east of S Kitley Ave and south of Julian Ave.
